heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Made for Edgware
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Edgware | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Edgware's 1930s semis. Designed around your home, built in weeks, installed in days. Kitchens, offices and more.
intro:
Edgware grew up around the Northern line, and it shows in the best possible way: street after street of solid 1930s semis with generous gardens and room to grow. If you love where you live but you've simply run out of space, extending almost always makes more sense than moving — especially when the alternative means leaving good schools, family nearby and a Tube station at the end of the road.

That's where we come in. EazyBase designs and delivers modular home extensions in Edgware that are precision-built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks, then installed at your home in under a week. A new kitchen-diner, a proper home office, a playroom, a garden room — finished to a standard that feels like it was always part of the house.

Because the build happens off-site, you skip months of skips, scaffolding and mud. Your garden stays intact, your neighbours stay friendly, and your family carries on living normally while your extension takes shape 200 miles away.

localAngles.heading: Extending a 1930s Edgware semi
localAngles.body: The classic Edgware semi is a wonderful starting point: a wide plot, a decent rear garden and a layout that responds beautifully to a rear extension. Opening up the back of the house into a full-width kitchen-diner transforms how these homes work day to day, pulling light deep into rooms that were designed for a different era. Many local homeowners pair a loft conversion with a rear extension — the loft adds bedrooms, while a modular rear extension delivers the open living space downstairs. Because our modules arrive largely complete, the two projects don't have to turn your home into a building site for a year.

localAngles.heading: Planning across the Barnet and Harrow boundary
localAngles.body: Edgware sits astride two boroughs — most of the town falls under Barnet, while streets on the Canons Park side come under Harrow — so the first job is confirming which council covers your address. The good news is that permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, meaning many projects need no full planning application at all; always check with your council before committing. Where planning permission is needed, our design team prepares the drawings and manages the application for you, and a factory-built extension is assessed just like any other — it's the size and siting that matter, not how it's constructed.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works so well here
localAngles.body: Edgware's residential streets weren't laid out with six months of builders' vans in mind. Driveways are shared, parking is contested, and nobody wants a cement mixer outside the front window until next spring. Factory building flips the equation: the messy, weather-dependent work happens in Blackburn, and what arrives at your home is a precise, quality-checked structure that our team installs in under a week. Less disruption for you, less strain on the street, and a build quality that hand-built extensions in a wet London winter struggle to match. For busy households near the Tube, it's the difference between a project and an ordeal.

localAngles.heading: Space for every generation
localAngles.body: Edgware is a town where families put down roots — and increasingly, where several generations share one roof. Grown-up children saving for their own place, grandparents moving closer, relatives visiting for weeks at a time: the pressure on space is real. A modular extension is one of the most practical answers. A garden room gives a student somewhere quiet to study, a rear extension creates a kitchen big enough for the whole family to gather, and a well-designed ground-floor room can give an older relative comfort and independence. One structure, built around how your family actually lives.

faqs.q: How long would an EazyBase extension take for a home in Edgware?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks while your foundations are prepared at home. Once it's ready, delivery and installation at your Edgware property typically take under a week. Compared with a traditional build, you spend far less time living alongside the work — most of the project happens before we ever park a vehicle on your street.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Edgware?
faqs.a: It depends on your address and the design. Most of Edgware falls under Barnet Council, with some streets under Harrow, and permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions — always check with your council first. As part of every project, we assess your property, advise whether an application is needed and handle the drawings and paperwork if it is.

faqs.q: Can you deliver a module to a typical Edgware street?
faqs.a: In almost all cases, yes. We carry out a site survey early on to check access, parking and any overhead obstructions, and we plan the delivery around your street — including quieter times of day where that helps. Edgware's 1930s semis, with their driveways and side access, are generally well suited to modular installation, and we confirm everything before you commit.

faqs.q: What do Edgware homeowners typically use their extensions for?
faqs.a: Open-plan kitchen-diners are the most popular choice, especially paired with a loft conversion to add bedrooms upstairs. Home offices are close behind for those working from home part of the week, and garden rooms, playrooms and flexible family spaces are increasingly common in multigenerational households looking for extra breathing room.
